heading: Floating drydock for United States Coast Guard Yard
label: 1159 Floating drydock for United States Coast Guard Yard

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), the Commandant, using funds appropriated pursuant to section 1181, may not acquire, procure, or construct a floating dry dock for the Coast Guard Yard.

children.heading: .—Permissible Acquisition, Procurement, or Construction Methods
children.label: b .—Permissible Acquisition, Procurement, or Construction Methods

children.text: provide for an entity other than the Coast Guard to contract for the acquisition, procurement, or construction of a floating drydock by contract, purchase, or other agreement;
construct a floating drydock at the Coast Guard Yard; or
acquire or procure a commercially available floating drydock.

children.heading: .—Design Standards and Construction Practices
children.label: c .—Design Standards and Construction Practices
children.text: To the extent practicable, a floating drydock acquired, procured, or constructed under this section shall reflect commercial design standards and commercial construction practices that are consistent with the best interests of the Federal Government.

children.heading: .—Berthing Requirement
children.label: d .—Berthing Requirement
children.text: Any floating drydock acquired, procured, or constructed under subsection (b) shall be berthed at the Coast Guard Yard in Baltimore, Maryland, when lifting or maintaining vessels.
constructed in the United States; and
capable of meeting the lifting and maintenance requirements of an Offshore Patrol Cutter or a National Security Cutter.
